creator: yehorov@gmail.com
text:
We have FreeBSD 9.2-STABLE installed on Dell PowerEdge R620 with PERC H710 Mini RAID contoller.
On high I/O load the system becomes unresponsive and the following messages appear in dmesg.
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=585938796544, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=585991651328, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=585999679488, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586085498880, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586089299968, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586090577920, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586094182400, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586095067136, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586095820800, length=32768)]error = 11
g_vfs_done():mfid0p5[WRITE(offset=586096672768, length=32768)]error = 11

Some days ago the system crashed.
# kgdb /boot/kernel/kernel.symbols /usr/crash/vmcore.0

There is absolutely no warranty for GDB.  Type "show warranty" for details.
